          connector: smtp
          operation: send_email
          config: ''
          params:
            to_recipients: test@example.com
            subject: Hello from FortiSOAR

        next: End

rows.yaml:
collection: default
playbooks:
  - name: Severity Router
    description: Route based on alert severity parameter
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        arguments:
          button_label: Route Alert
      - type: decision
        name: Check Severity
        conditions:
          - display: High Severity
            when: "{{ vars.input.params.severity == 'High' }}"
            next: High Severity End
          - display: Other
            default: true
            next: Other Severity End
      - type: end
        name: High Severity End
      - type: end
        name: Other Severity End

rows.yaml:
collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: Fetch and Reference Alerts
    description: Demonstrates find_record with downstream set_variable using hydra member reference
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        next: Fetch Alerts
      - type: find_record
        name: Fetch Alerts
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          query:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: name
                operator: contains
                value: phishing
          partial: true
        next: Process Results
      - type: set_variable
        name: Process Results
        vars:
          alert_records: "{{ vars.steps.Fetch_Alerts['hydra:member'] }}"
          alert_count: "{{ vars.steps.Fetch_Alerts['hydra:member'] | length }}"
          alert_names: "{{ vars.steps.Fetch_Alerts['hydra:member'] | map(attribute='name') | list }}"
        message:
          content: "Fetched {{ vars.alert_count }} phishing alerts. First alert names: {{ vars.alert_names }}"
          tags:
            - phishing
            - automated
        next: End
      - type: end
        name: End

rows.yaml:
collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: Triage Alert
    description: Triage an alert based on severity and update verdict field
    parameters: []
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          button_label: Triage Alert
          run_mode: per_record
        next: Check Severity
      - type: decision
        name: Check Severity
        conditions:
          - display: High Severity
            when: "{{ vars.input.records[0].severity == 'High' }}"
            next: Set High Verdict
          - display: Else
            default: true
            next: Set Low Verdict
      - type: update_record
        name: Set High Verdict
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          collection: "{{ vars.input.records[0]['@id'] }}"
          resource:
            verdict: High Risk - Requires Investigation
        next: End Playbook
      - type: update_record
        name: Set Low Verdict
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          collection: "{{ vars.input.records[0]['@id'] }}"
          resource:
            verdict: Low Priority - Monitor
        next: End Playbook
      - type: end
        name: End Playbook

rows.yaml:
collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: Alert Status Change Handler
    description: Fires when an alert status changes and updates resolution when status becomes Closed
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start_on_update
        name: Alert Status Changed
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: status
                op: changed
      - type: decision
        name: Check If Closed
        conditions:
          - display: Status is Closed
            when: "{{ vars.input.records[0].status == 'Closed' }}"
            next: Update Resolution
          - display: Else
            default: true
            next: End
      - type: update_record
        name: Update Resolution
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          collection: "{{ vars.input.records[0]['@id'] }}"
          resource:
            resolution: Alert has been closed
      - type: end
        name: End

rows.yaml:
collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: Batch Create Alerts
    description: Create a static list of alert payloads using for_each iteration
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        next: Build Alert List
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Build Alert List
        vars:
          alerts:
            - name: Suspicious Login Attempt
              description: Login detected from unusual location
              severity: '{{ "High" | picklist("severity") }}'
              sourceIp: 192.168.1.100
              destinationIp: 10.0.0.50
            - name: Malware Detection
              description: Potential malware identified in file scan
              severity: '{{ "Critical" | picklist("severity") }}'
              sourceIp: 203.0.113.45
              destinationIp: 10.0.0.75
            - name: Policy Violation
              description: Data exfiltration attempt detected
              severity: '{{ "Medium" | picklist("severity") }}'
              sourceIp: 198.51.100.20
              destinationIp: 10.0.0.100
        next: Create Alerts Loop
      
      - type: for_each
        name: Create Alerts Loop
        arguments:
          list: '{{ vars.alerts }}'
          steps:
            - type: create_record
              name: Create Alert Record
              arguments:
                module: alerts
                resource:
                  name: '{{ vars.item.name }}'
                  description: '{{ vars.item.description }}'
                  severity: '{{ vars.item.severity }}'
                  sourceIp: '{{ vars.item.sourceIp }}'
                  destinationIp: '{{ vars.item.destinationIp }}'
        next: Complete
      
      - type: end
        name: Complete

collection: Demo
playbooks:
  - name: Manual Approval Decision
    description: Manual trigger that pauses for analyst approval, then routes to different verdict steps
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        next: Request Approval

      - type: manual_input
        name: Request Approval
        arguments:
          title: Review and Approve
          description: Please review the details and select approve or reject.
        options:
          - display: Approve
            primary: true
            next: Set Approved Verdict
          - display: Reject
            next: Set Rejected Verdict

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Approved Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: approved
          timestamp: '{{ now() }}'
        message:
          content: Request has been approved
          tags:
            - approved
            - automation

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Rejected Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: rejected
          timestamp: '{{ now() }}'
        message:
          content: Request has been rejected
          tags:
            - rejected
            - automation

      - type: end
        name: End Approved Path

      - type: end
        name: End Rejected Path

rows.yaml:
collection: Demo Collection
playbooks:
  - name: Find and Update Alert Severity
    description: Manually finds an alert by name and updates its severity field
    parameters:
      - alert_name
      - new_severity
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        next: Find Alert By Name
      - type: find_record
        name: Find Alert By Name
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          query:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: name
                operator: eq
                value: '{{ vars.input.params.alert_name }}'
          partial: true
        next: Check Result
      - type: decision
        name: Check Result
        conditions:
          - display: Alert Found
            when: '{{ vars.steps.Find_Alert_By_Name["hydra:member"] | length > 0 }}'
            next: Update Alert Severity
        default_branch:
          display: No Alert Found
          next: Done
      - type: update_record
        name: Update Alert Severity
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          collection: '{{ vars.steps.Find_Alert_By_Name["hydra:member"][0]["@id"] }}'
          resource:
            severity: '{{ vars.input.params.new_severity | picklist("severity") }}'
        next: Done
      - type: end
        name: Done

rows.yaml:
collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: VirusTotal IP Reputation Check
    description: Query VirusTotal for IP reputation and branch on malicious analysis count
    parameters:
      - ip
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        arguments:
          button_label: Check IP Reputation
        next: Query VirusTotal

      - type: connector
        name: Query VirusTotal
        arguments:
          connector: virustotal
          operation: query_ip
          config: ''
          params:
            ip: '{{ vars.input.params.ip }}'
        next: Check Malicious Count

      - type: decision
        name: Check Malicious Count
        conditions:
          - display: High Risk
            when: '{{ vars.steps.Query_VirusTotal.get("last_analysis_stats", {}).get("malicious", 0) > 5 }}'
            next: Set High Risk Verdict
          - display: Medium Risk
            when: '{{ vars.steps.Query_VirusTotal.get("last_analysis_stats", {}).get("malicious", 0) > 0 }}'
            next: Set Medium Risk Verdict
        default_branch:
          display: Clean
          next: Set Clean Verdict

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set High Risk Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: HIGH_RISK
          malicious_count: '{{ vars.steps.Query_VirusTotal.get("last_analysis_stats", {}).get("malicious", 0) }}'
        message:
          content: 'IP {{ vars.input.params.ip }} flagged as HIGH RISK with {{ vars.malicious_count }} malicious detections'
          tags: [virustotal, threat_intelligence]
        next: End

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Medium Risk Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: MEDIUM_RISK
          malicious_count: '{{ vars.steps.Query_VirusTotal.get("last_analysis_stats", {}).get("malicious", 0) }}'
        message:
          content: 'IP {{ vars.input.params.ip }} flagged as MEDIUM RISK with {{ vars.malicious_count }} malicious detections'
          tags: [virustotal, threat_intelligence]
        next: End

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Clean Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: CLEAN
          malicious_count: 0
        message:
          content: 'IP {{ vars.input.params.ip }} is clean with no malicious detections'
          tags: [virustotal, threat_intelligence]
        next: End

      - type: end
        name: End

collection: FortiSOAR
playbooks:
  - name: Confirm Before Block
    description: Manual trigger that prompts SOC analyst to approve or reject a block action
    is_active: true

        name: Run
        next: Ask For Approval

      - type: manual_input
        name: Ask For Approval
        arguments:
          title: Confirm Block Action
          description: Review the details and confirm whether to proceed with the block.
        options:
          - display: Approve
            primary: true
            next: Record Approval
          - display: Reject
            next: Record Rejection

      - type: set_variable

          action_status: approved
          action_timestamp: "{{ now() }}"

          content: Block action has been approved
          tags:
            - automation
            - block_approved

      - type: set_variable
        name: Record Rejection
        vars:
          action_status: rejected
          action_timestamp: "{{ now() }}"
        message:
          content: Block action has been rejected
          tags:
            - automation
            - block_rejected

      - type: end
        name: End Approval

rows.yaml:
collection: FortiSOAR
playbooks:
  - name: IP Analysis and Action
    description: Prompt analyst to enter an IP and choose action (block or allow)
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run

      - type: manual_input
        name: IP Analysis Request
        arguments:
          title: IP Address Analysis
          description: Enter the IP address and select an action to perform.
          inputs:
            - name: ip_address
              kind: ipv4
              label: IP Address
              required: true
            - name: action
              kind: select
              label: Action
              required: true
              options:
                - block
                - allow
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Block IP Address
          - display: Allow
            next: Log Allow Decision

      - type: connector
        name: Block IP Address
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip_new
          config: ''
          params:
            method: Quarantine Based
            ip_addresses: '{{ vars.steps.IP_Analysis_Request.input.ip_address }}'
            time_to_live: 12 Hour
        next: Log Block Decision

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Block Decision
        vars:
          action_taken: block
          ip_blocked: '{{ vars.steps.IP_Analysis_Request.input.ip_address }}'
        message:
          content: 'IP address {{ vars.steps.IP_Analysis_Request.input.ip_address }} has been blocked on FortiGate.'
          tags:
            - block
            - automated
        next: End

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Allow Decision
        vars:
          action_taken: allow
          ip_allowed: '{{ vars.steps.IP_Analysis_Request.input.ip_address }}'
        message:
          content: 'IP address {{ vars.steps.IP_Analysis_Request.input.ip_address }} was allowed. No action taken.'
          tags:
            - allow
            - automated
        next: End

      - type: end
        name: End

collection: Phishing Response
playbooks:
  - name: Phishing IP Reputation and Block
    description: >
      On phishing alert creation, check sender IPs against VirusTotal.
      For high-reputation IPs (score > 50), prompt analyst to block via FortiGate.
      Post summary comment to alert.
    steps:
      - type: start_on_create
        name: Phishing Alert Created
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: tags
                op: contains
                value: phishing

      - type: set_variable
        name: Initialize Variables
        vars:
          alert_id: '{{ vars.input.records[0]["@id"] }}'
          alert_name: '{{ vars.input.records[0].name }}'
          sender_ips: '{{ vars.input.records[0].sender_ips | default([]) }}'
          high_score_ips: []
          blocked_ips: []
          reputation_threshold: 50

      - type: connector
        name: Check IP Reputation
        arguments:
          connector: virustotal
          operation: query_ip
          config: ''
          params:
            ip: '{{ item }}'
        foreach:
          item: '{{ vars.sender_ips }}'
          save_context: true

      - type: set_variable
        name: Collect High Score IPs
        vars:
          high_score_ips: |
            {%- set high_ips = [] -%}
            {%- for check_result in vars.steps.Check_IP_Reputation -%}
              {%- if check_result.reputation | int > vars.reputation_threshold | int -%}
                {%- set _ = high_ips.append({'ip': check_result.ip, 'reputation': check_result.reputation}) -%}
              {%- endif -%}
            {%- endfor -%}
            {{ high_ips }}

      - type: decision
        name: Any High Score IPs
        conditions:
          - display: 'Yes, high-score IPs found'
            when: '{{ vars.high_score_ips | length > 0 }}'
            next: Ask Analyst to Block
          - display: 'No high-score IPs'
            default: true
            next: Post Summary Comment

      - type: manual_input
        name: Ask Analyst to Block
        arguments:
          title: 'High-Reputation IPs Detected'
          description: |
            The following sender IPs have reputation scores above {{ vars.reputation_threshold }}:
            {% for ip_info in vars.high_score_ips %}
            - {{ ip_info.ip }}: Score {{ ip_info.reputation }}
            {% endfor %}
            
            Proceed with blocking these IPs on FortiGate?
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Block IPs on FortiGate
          - display: Skip
            next: Post Summary Comment

      - type: connector
        name: Block IPs on FortiGate
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: create_address
          config: ''
          params:
            address_category: IPv4 Address
            name: 'Blocked-{{ item.ip }}-Phishing'
            type: Subnet
            subnet: '{{ item.ip }}/32'
            comment: 'Phishing alert: {{ vars.alert_name }}'
        foreach:
          item: '{{ vars.high_score_ips }}'
          save_context: true

      - type: set_variable
        name: Record Blocked IPs
        vars:
          blocked_ips: '{{ vars.high_score_ips | map(attribute="ip") | list }}'

      - type: set_variable
        name: Post Summary Comment
        vars:
          final_status: completed
        message:
          content: |
            **Phishing IP Reputation Check Summary**
            
            Alert: {{ vars.alert_name }}
            Total sender IPs checked: {{ vars.sender_ips | length }}
            IPs with high reputation score (>{{ vars.reputation_threshold }}): {{ vars.high_score_ips | length }}
            
            {% if vars.high_score_ips | length > 0 %}
            **Detailed Results:**
            {% for ip_info in vars.high_score_ips %}
            - {{ ip_info.ip }}: Reputation Score {{ ip_info.reputation }}
            {% endfor %}
            {% endif %}
            
            {% if vars.blocked_ips | length > 0 %}
            **Action Taken:** {{ vars.blocked_ips | length }} IP(s) blocked on FortiGate
            Blocked IPs: {{ vars.blocked_ips | join(', ') }}
            {% else %}
            **Action Taken:** No IPs were blocked (either no high-score IPs or analyst chose to skip).
            {% endif %}
          tags:
            - phishing_response
            - automated

      - type: end
        name: End

collection: Demo
playbooks:
  - name: Auto-Remediate Disk Full Alert
    description: Handle disk_full alerts by running cleanup, checking free space, and escalating if needed
    is_active: true
    parameters: []
    steps:
      - type: start_on_create
        name: Alert Created
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: category
                op: eq
                value: disk_full
        next: Extract Alert Details
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Extract Alert Details
        vars:
          affected_ip: '{{ vars.input.records[0].affected_host }}'
          alert_id: '{{ vars.input.records[0]["@id"] }}'
          alert_name: '{{ vars.input.records[0].name }}'
        next: Find Host Record
      
      - type: find_record
        name: Find Host Record
        arguments:
          module: assets
          query:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: ipv4
                operator: eq
                value: '{{ vars.affected_ip }}'
          partial: true
        next: Check Host Found
      
      - type: decision
        name: Check Host Found
        conditions:
          - display: Host Found
            when: '{{ vars.steps.Find_Host_Record["hydra:member"] | length > 0 }}'
            next: Extract Host Info
        default_branch:
          display: Host Not Found
          next: Skip Cleanup
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Extract Host Info
        vars:
          host_ip: '{{ vars.steps.Find_Host_Record["hydra:member"][0].ipv4 }}'
          host_name: '{{ vars.steps.Find_Host_Record["hydra:member"][0].name }}'
        next: Run Cleanup Job
      
      - type: connector
        name: Run Cleanup Job
        arguments:
          connector: ssh
          operation: run_remote_command
          config: ''
          params:
            cmd: 'ansible-playbook /opt/playbooks/clean_tmp.yml -i {{ vars.host_ip }}'
            is_super_user: 'true'
        next: Check Free Space
      
      - type: connector
        name: Check Free Space
        arguments:
          connector: ssh
          operation: run_remote_command
          config: ''
          params:
            cmd: 'ansible-playbook /opt/playbooks/check_disk.yml -i {{ vars.host_ip }} | grep "free_space_percent" | awk ''{print $NF}'''
        next: Save Free Space Value
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Save Free Space Value
        vars:
          free_space_percent: '{{ vars.steps.Check_Free_Space.stdout | float }}'
        next: Evaluate Free Space
      
      - type: decision
        name: Evaluate Free Space
        conditions:
          - display: Free Space Below 10%
            when: '{{ vars.free_space_percent < 10 }}'
            next: Page On Call
        default_branch:
          display: Free Space Acceptable
          next: Mark Alert Resolved
      
      - type: connector
        name: Page On Call
        arguments:
          connector: pagerduty
          operation: create_incident
          config: ''
          params:
            from: 'alerts@company.com'
            title: 'URGENT: Disk cleanup failed on {{ vars.host_name }}'
            description: 'Host {{ vars.host_name }} ({{ vars.host_ip }}) still has only {{ vars.free_space_percent }}% free space after auto-cleanup attempt.'
            priority: Priority 1
            urgency: High
        next: Log Escalation
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Escalation
        vars: {}
        message:
          content: 'Escalated to on-call: cleanup completed but disk still critically low at {{ vars.free_space_percent }}% free space on {{ vars.host_name }}'
          tags:
            - disk_full
            - escalation
        next: End
      
      - type: update_record
        name: Mark Alert Resolved
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          collection: '{{ vars.alert_id }}'
          resource:
            status: Closed
        next: Log Resolution
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Resolution
        vars: {}
        message:
          content: 'Auto-cleanup successful on {{ vars.host_name }}. Free space recovered to {{ vars.free_space_percent }}%.'
          tags:
            - disk_full
            - auto_remediation
        next: End
      
      - type: set_variable
        name: Skip Cleanup
        vars: {}
        message:
          content: 'Alert {{ vars.alert_name }} for IP {{ vars.affected_ip }} could not be remediated: host record not found in assets module.'
          tags:
            - disk_full
            - remediation_failed
        next: End
      
      - type: end
        name: End

collection: Default
playbooks:
  - name: NOC Ticket SLA Escalation with PagerDuty Page
    description: Monitors NOC tickets for SLA breach (sla_pct >= 80) with High/Critical severity. Pages on-call via PagerDuty, waits 15 min, then escalates severity and pages manager if still unacknowledged.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start_on_update
        name: Run
        arguments:
          module: tickets
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: sla_pct
                op: gte
                value: 80
              - field: severity
                op: in
                value:
                  - High
                  - Critical

      - type: set_variable
        name: Stash Ticket Details
        vars:
          ticket_id: '{{ vars.input.records[0].id }}'
          ticket_iri: '{{ vars.input.records[0]["@id"] }}'
          ticket_name: '{{ vars.input.records[0].name }}'
          ticket_severity: '{{ vars.input.records[0].severity }}'

      - type: connector
        name: Page On Call
        arguments:
          connector: pagerduty
          operation: send_event
          config: ''
          params:
            summary: 'NOC TICKET SLA BREACH - {{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_name }}'
            source: 'FortiSOAR Ticket #{{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_id }}'
            severity: Critical
            class: ticket_escalation

      - type: delay
        name: Wait 15 Minutes
        arguments:
          minutes: 15

      - type: find_record
        name: Fetch Ticket
        arguments:
          module: tickets
          query:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: '@id'
                operator: eq
                value: '{{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_iri }}'
          partial: true

      - type: decision
        name: Acknowledged
        conditions:
          - display: Still Not Acknowledged
            when: "vars.steps.Fetch_Ticket['hydra:member'] | length > 0 and vars.steps.Fetch_Ticket['hydra:member'][0].acknowledged_at == null"
            next: Page Manager

      - type: end
        name: End

      - type: connector
        name: Page Manager
        arguments:
          connector: pagerduty
          operation: send_event
          config: ''
          params:
            summary: 'ESCALATION - NOC TICKET STILL UNACKNOWLEDGED - {{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_name }}'
            source: 'FortiSOAR Ticket #{{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_id }}'
            severity: Critical
            class: manager_escalation

      - type: set_variable
        name: Determine New Severity
        vars:
          new_severity: '{% if vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_severity == "High" %}{{ "Critical" | picklist("Severity") }}{% else %}{{ "Critical" | picklist("Severity") }}{% endif %}'
          escalation_message: '{% if vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_severity == "Critical" %}Already at top severity{% else %}Escalated from High to Critical due to SLA breach and non-acknowledgment{% endif %}'

      - type: update_record
        name: Update Ticket Severity
        arguments:
          module: tickets
          collection: '{{ vars.steps.Stash_Ticket_Details.ticket_iri }}'
          resource:
            severity: '{{ vars.steps.Determine_New_Severity.new_severity }}'
        message:
          content: '{{ vars.steps.Determine_New_Severity.escalation_message }}'
          tags:
            - sla_breach
            - escalation

      - type: end
        name: Final End

rows.yaml:
collection: FortiSOAR Default Collection
playbooks:
  - name: Akamai Block URL via Network Lists
    description: Manually block a URL via Akamai Network Lists API using HTTP connector
    parameters:
      - akamai_bearer_token
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Run
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          button_label: Block URL in Akamai
          run_mode: per_record
        next: Get URL from Analyst

      - type: manual_input
        name: Get URL from Analyst
        arguments:
          title: Block URL in Akamai Network Lists
          description: Enter the URL you want to block in Akamai. This will add it to your Network List.
          inputs:
            - name: url_to_block
              kind: url
              label: URL to Block
              required: true
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: POST to Akamai Network Lists
          - display: Cancel
            next: End

      - type: connector
        name: POST to Akamai Network Lists
        arguments:
          connector: http
          operation: http_post
          config: ''
          params:
            rest_api: 'https://configapi.akamai.com/network-list/v2/lists'
            header: |
              {
                "Authorization": "Bearer {{ vars.input.params.akamai_bearer_token }}",
                "Content-Type": "application/json"
              }
            data: |
              {
                "listItems": ["{{ vars.steps.Get_URL_from_Analyst.input.url_to_block }}"]
              }
        next: Comment Result

      - type: set_variable
        name: Comment Result
        vars:
          api_status: '{{ vars.steps.POST_to_Akamai_Network_Lists.status }}'
          api_response: '{{ vars.steps.POST_to_Akamai_Network_Lists | json_encode }}'
        message:
          content: |
            **Akamai Network Lists API Block Result**
            
            **URL Blocked**: {{ vars.steps.Get_URL_from_Analyst.input.url_to_block }}
            **API Status**: {{ vars.steps.POST_to_Akamai_Network_Lists.status }}
            **Response**: {{ vars.steps.POST_to_Akamai_Network_Lists | json_encode }}
          tags:
            - akamai_block
            - automation

      - type: end
        name: End
